fortune_decode: Agent second brain. 8 sessions distilled. Latest: 2026-05-12. Projects: repo-copilot, agent-feed-optimization. Top concepts: AFO website audit product, LLM visibility audit service, before/after proof demo, $10-20/month monitor tier, audit-fix-monitor business model, AFO content-layer proof, SEO analogy confirmed, G-000 gap report v0.3 needed, Scenario B confounder (GitHub connector), bootstrap URL pattern.

notes.title: AFO Website Audit Product — accidentally-on-purpose product concept
new_concepts: AFO gap report as client deliverable
notes.decisions: The AFO test suite IS the audit engine. G-000 running a URL = the audit. The gap report output = the client deliverable. Business model: (1) Audit — free lead magnet or $49 one-time, run URL through G-000 gap report; (2) Fix — build missing files (rss.xml, llms.txt, agent-context, policy, actions, context-cookie) for $200-500 flat; (3) Monitor — re-run audit monthly, push updates as LLM standards evolve, $10-20/month; (4) Proof — before/after test showing client's site appearing in LLM answers, included in monitor tier. The killer demo: open ChatGPT/Perplexity, ask about the client's niche, show their site appearing in the answer. Same question before and after. Screenshot pair = entire sales pitch. Defensible because ROI is same-day and self-verifiable by the client — unlike SEO which takes months. G-000 v0.3 gap report patch = the audit report template. We were building the product the whole time.
notes.open_questions: What is the minimum viable audit report format for a non-technical client? Should the audit be fully automated (URL in → PDF out) or agent-assisted? What is the fulfillment workflow — does Jared build the files manually, or does an agent generate them? Is the primary customer a solo website owner, a small business, or an agency reseller?

notes.title: AFO Scenario B: 3-way test results + 4 findings
notes.decisions: Scenario B 3-way complete. Finding 1: GitHub connector neutralizes baseline gap — both agents score similarly when connector is on. Finding 2: Plain Perplexity no-connector spontaneously offered to build AFO files for the repo — unprompted gap-to-offer behavior. Finding 3: G-000 Space does NOT produce structured gap report for non-AFO sites; plain baseline did it better. G-000 v0.3 needs: per-endpoint checkmark status + 'adding this enables X' + estimated AFO score/18. Finding 4: AFO terminology in LLM training data — plain Perplexity web-searched its way to sanity.io field guide and AFO-adjacent concepts without any instructions. Connector state must be controlled variable in all future tests. Canonical test condition = no connector.
notes.open_questions: Does partial AFO (llms.txt only) produce measurable baseline lift? What is minimum viable AFO surface?

notes.title: AFO Scenario C: protocol works at content layer — key finding
notes.decisions: Baseline 14/18, AFO Space 17/18. Close gap = strong content. SEO analogy confirmed. Any LLM surfaces AFO-structured content better without special instructions.
notes.open_questions: Does Scenario B delta hold for partial AFO sites?

notes.title: archive_block op added to notion-ops
notes.decisions: Added archive_block op — PATCH /blocks/{id} with archived:true; unarchive boolean supported
notes.open_questions: Should we also add a dedicated archive_database alias op for clarity?

notes.title: Cleanup audit — 3 Alice items resolved
notes.decisions: Items 1 and 3 already clean; Item 2 (archive old DB) needs Jared action in Notion
notes.open_questions: Should we add archive_database op to notion-ops?

notes.title: Full build order + roadmap + Bob message sent
notes.decisions: Space instructions = one bootstrap URL. Build order finalized across 4 phases.
notes.open_questions: Provision Action design: GitHub App vs PAT?

notes.decisions: Every Alice turn ends with append_note as slot 3.

notes.title: gitzip hardening + Path A: Agent Notes DB live
notes.decisions: append_note is canonical session-end write primitive.
